commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=2491f6e82fd23e37417a4a060771172dcd4af0eb)
commands.review_summary:
This PR changes Gateway MCP loopback schema projection and call dispatch to skip malformed tool metadata, reject calls to omitted tools, and add focused regression tests.

PR surface: Source +98, Tests +378. Total +476 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main directly dereferences loopback tool metadata, and the PR adds a focused test path with throwing getters and malformed schemas; I did not execute tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Maintainer review should decide the compatibility behavior for malformed plugin-owned loopback tools; there is no narrow automated repair blocker until that decision is made.

target.title: fix(gateway): harden MCP loopback tool schemas
target.body:
## Summary

- hardens Gateway MCP loopback tool listing against unreadable or malformed tool names, descriptions, and parameter schemas
- keeps healthy sibling tools listed and callable when malformed loopback tools are skipped
- makes `tools/call` use the same safely advertised schema surface so omitted tools are not executed through a malformed call path
- uses only synthetic `fuzzplugin` / `mockplugin` fixtures in tests and PR notes

## Verification

- replayed the narrow gateway slice onto current `origin/main` 5c5711f061cd after the old PR branch became conflicting
- `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/mcp-http.test.ts --reporter=verbose`: 1 Gateway shard, 27 tests passed on head 2491f6e82fd2
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/gateway/mcp-http.handlers.ts src/gateway/mcp-http.schema.ts src/gateway/mcp-http.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/gateway/mcp-http.handlers.ts src/gateway/mcp-http.schema.ts src/gateway/mcp-http.test.ts`
- `git diff --check` plus touched-file scrub for reported fixture names and private local paths: clean
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`: clean, no accepted/actionable findings

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: malformed Gateway MCP loopback tool schemas no longer crash tool-list projection or leave a skipped/invalid tool callable through `tools/call`.

Real environment tested: linked OpenClaw `gwt` worktree on current `origin/main` 5c5711f061cd with targeted Gateway MCP loopback tests on head 2491f6e82fd2.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: replayed the narrow gateway slice onto current main, resolved the import-boundary conflict, ran the focused Gateway MCP test file, formatter check, targeted lint check, diff check, touched-file scrub, and autoreview.

Evidence after fix: tests prove unreadable loopback tool names/descriptions/parameter schemas are skipped without dropping healthy siblings, malformed union/property schemas do not crash listing, and calls to invalid or safely omitted tools are not executed.

Observed result after fix: Gateway MCP loopback clients get a safe tool list and healthy tools continue to work even when one loopback tool exposes bad schema metadata.

What was not tested: no fresh remote `pnpm check:changed` after this current-main replay; no full live MCP client round trip. Behavior is covered by Gateway MCP loopback tests.
target.author: vincentkoc
